opportunities.title: [OSS] shamilahmdt/devtasks: 🟢 Good First Issue: Implement Persistent Global Dark Mode with Universal Toggle 🌙
opportunities.description:
# 🟢 Good First Issue: Implement Persistent Global Dark Mode with Universal Toggle 🌙

**Time:** ~20–40 minutes
**Difficulty:** Beginner / Intermediate
**Skill Level:** First-time contributors welcome

🟢 This issue is perfect for contributors who want hands-on experience with:

* React global state
* Context API
* localStorage persistence
* reusable components
* theme management

---

## 🏷 Labels

`good first issue` · `help wanted` · `feature` · `dark-mode` · `react` · `ui-improvement` · `hacktoberfest`

**Tech Stack:** React, Tailwind CSS, React Icons
**Good For:** Contributors learning reusabl

opportunities.title: [OSS] OMNYX-OS/OMNYX-OS: Write unit tests for the risk scoring and tracker detection engines
opportunities.description:
## Summary

\`src/services/riskEngine.ts\` and \`src/services/trackerDetector.ts\` contain the core intelligence logic for OMNYX -- permission scoring, risk tier assignment, tracker pattern matching -- but have zero tests. These are pure functions with no React, no native code, and no store dependencies, making them ideal for unit testing.

## What to build

Write Jest unit tests covering the main behaviors of both engines.

## Setup

\`\`\`bash
npm install --save-dev jest @types/jest ts-jest --legacy-peer-deps
\`\`\`

Add a \`jest.config.js\` at the project root:

\`\`\`js
module.exports = {

opportunities.title: [OSS] OMNYX-OS/OMNYX-OS: Add Privacy Report share sheet after scan
opportunities.description:
## Summary

After scanning, users have no way to save or share their results. A plain-text privacy report via the native share sheet would let users document their findings or show them to others.

## What to build

Add a "Share Report" button to the scan result card in \`app/(tabs)/intelligence.tsx\` (the card that appears after a scan completes). Use React Native's built-in \`Share.share()\` API -- no library needed.

## Report format

\`\`\`
OMNYX Privacy Report
Scanned: [date and time]
Apps analyzed: [count]
Privacy score: [score]/100

Risk breakdown:
  Critical: [count]
  High: [count]

opportunities.title: [OSS] ghostfolio/ghostfolio: Improve pagination for activities in holding detail dialog
opportunities.description:
The [holding detail dialog](https://github.com/ghostfolio/ghostfolio/tree/main/apps/client/src/app/components/holding-detail-dialog) currently handles pagination entirely on the client as it fetches the entire activity history from the backend at once. For large portfolios, this causes a performance bottleneck.

Update the frontend API call to pass `take` and `skip` query parameters to the backend.

opportunities.title: [OSS] ghostfolio/ghostfolio: Improve pagination for activities in account detail dialog
opportunities.description:

For large portfolios, this causes a performance bottleneck.

Update the frontend API call to pass `take` and `skip` query parameters to the backend.

opportunities.title: [OSS] OMNYX-OS/OMNYX-OS: Add permission explanation tooltip in Risk App List
opportunities.description:
## Summary

When users see permission names like \`READ_CONTACTS\` or \`BIND_ACCESSIBILITY_SERVICE\` in the Risk App List, they have no context for whether it is dangerous or why.

## What to build

Add a tap-to-expand tooltip or modal on each permission name inside \`AppRiskCard\` in \`app/(tabs)/intelligence.tsx\`. The permission descriptions already exist in \`src/constants/permissionDatabase.ts\` -- surface them in the UI.

## Files to touch

- \`app/(tabs)/intelligence.tsx\` -- add tap handler and expanded state to \`AppRiskCard\`
- \`src/constants/permissionDatabase.ts\` -- read descript

opportunities.title: [OSS] kubestellar/console: ⚡ perf: Virtualize dashboard card grid (150+ cards cause jank)
opportunities.description:
## Problem
`DashboardPage.tsx:426-447` renders all cards via `cards.map(...)` in a single grid — no windowing or virtualization. With 150+ cards this causes:
- High initial mount time
- Layout jank during scroll
- Unnecessary render cycles for offscreen cards

## Proposed Fix
- Use `react-virtuoso` or `react-window` for the card grid
- OR implement intersection-observer based lazy mounting (mount cards only when near viewport)
- Must integrate with existing drag-and-drop (`@dnd-kit`)

## Impact
HIGH — directly affects dashboard TTFI and scroll performance for power users.

## Files
- `web/src/

opportunities.title: [OSS] kubestellar/console: 🐛 api: GetCustomResources returns unbounded results (no pagination)
opportunities.description:
## Problem
`pkg/api/handlers/custom_resources.go:48-172` — `GetCustomResources` fans out to all clusters and accumulates all results in memory before responding. No pagination or result cap.

## Impact
HIGH — memory spikes and slow/timeout responses on large fleets (50+ clusters × thousands of CRs).

## Proposed Fix
- Add `limit` and `continue` query params
- Cap total result size (e.g., 500 items default, max 2000)
- Consider streaming via SSE for very large result sets

## Related
`pkg/api/handlers/mcp_query.go:31-83` has the same unbounded accumulation pattern in `queryAllClustersWithTimeou

opportunities.title: [OSS] kubestellar/console: 🌱 refactor: Extract demo-mode handler middleware (Go)
[OSS] kubestellar/console: ⚡ perf: Memoize DashboardPage/Services/Cost render-time allocations
opportunities.description:
## Problem
Several dashboard wrapper components recreate callbacks and objects on every render, causing prop identity churn and unnecessary child re-renders:

1. **DashboardPage.tsx:325-330, 412-415** — `getStatValue` and `emptyState.action` recreated every render
2. **Services.tsx:55-80** — `getDashboardStatValue` + inline `emptyState`/`secondaryAction` objects
3. **Cost.tsx:81-135** — `gpuByCluster` recomputed every render; `getDashboardStatValue` recreated

## Proposed Fix
- Wrap `getStatValue`/`getDashboardStatValue` in `useCallback`
- Wrap computed data (`gpuByCluster`) in `useMemo`
- Hoi
